skill.name: zentao-api
skill.description: Use ZenTao REST API v2.0 to query and manage projects, products, requirements, bugs, tasks, test cases, releases, users, and files.
skill.summary: Operate ZenTao project management records through documented REST API endpoints.
skill.author: easysoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis over-reported many Markdown backticks and weak-cryptography hits that are false positives in API documentation. However, manual review confirmed a high-risk eval workflow that emits unescaped credentials into the shell and a plaintext persistent token cache. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this is not a critical block.

high_findings.title: Shell Eval Command Injection in Token Setup
high_findings.description: The skill tells users to run the token helper through eval. The helper prints unescaped URL, token, and account values as shell assignments, so metacharacters from environment variables, the cache file, or a server response can execute in the user shell.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The eval instruction is explicit, and the script emits raw variable values without shell escaping. This creates a direct command execution path if any emitted value is attacker controlled.

high_findings.title: Persistent Plaintext ZenTao Token Cache
high_findings.description: The helper stores ZenTao URL, account, and token in a hidden home-directory JSON file. The documentation states ZenTao tokens are permanent, so local file disclosure can expose long-lived account access.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cache path and write operations are directly present, and the comments describe permanent token behavior. The risk depends on local file permissions but the credential persistence is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Network Requests and Mutating API Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to send authenticated requests to a user-configured ZenTao server and supports create, update, delete, and status transition actions. This is expected for the skill, but it can change project records and should require clear user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network call and many mutating curl examples are visible. The behavior is legitimate for this integration, but it raises operational risk when credentials are available.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: Most static external command hits in SKILL.md and api-reference.md are Markdown code spans, shell examples, endpoint paths, or API tables. They are not executable Ruby backticks in a source file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation text and tables, not Ruby code. The exception is the documented shell eval workflow, which is captured as a separate high-risk finding.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits map to API documentation terms, enum values, field names, and Markdown content. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, or custom cryptographic implementation in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of representative flagged lines shows API fields and status values rather than crypto operations. This makes the high static severity misleading for that pattern.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Heuristic Appears Caused by Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The high entropy heuristic points at normal shell script and Markdown files containing Chinese text and dense API references. No evidence found of encoded payloads, binary blobs, or encrypted content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged files are readable source and documentation. The confidence is below very high because entropy is a heuristic, but the reviewed context does not show obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Eval Command Injection in Token Setup
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells users to run the token helper through eval. The helper prints unescaped URL, token, and account values as shell assignments, so metacharacters from environment variables, the cache file, or a server response can execute in the user shell.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The eval instruction is explicit, and the script emits raw variable values without shell escaping. This creates a direct command execution path if any emitted value is attacker controlled.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Plaintext ZenTao Token Cache
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper stores ZenTao URL, account, and token in a hidden home-directory JSON file. The documentation states ZenTao tokens are permanent, so local file disclosure can expose long-lived account access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cache path and write operations are directly present, and the comments describe permanent token behavior. The risk depends on local file permissions but the credential persistence is confirmed.

content.user_title: Manage ZenTao Work Items Through API
content.value_statement: Teams need fast access to ZenTao records without memorizing every REST endpoint. This skill maps user requests to ZenTao API calls for projects, bugs, requirements, tasks, tests, releases, and users.
actual_capabilities: Looks up ZenTao projects, products, executions, bugs, tasks, requirements, and users through REST API v2.0.
Creates and updates work items such as bugs, stories, tasks, test cases, product plans, builds, releases, tickets, and feedback.
Runs status transitions for stories, bugs, tasks, feedback, and tickets when required fields are supplied.
Retrieves current item details before PUT updates when the API requires complete record data.
Uses ZenTao URL, account, password, or token values from environment variables or a local token cache.
Provides endpoint guidance from a local API reference for more than 20 ZenTao modules.
limitations: Requires access to a reachable ZenTao server and valid credentials or token.
Does not provide an official ZenTao SDK or typed client library.
Can perform destructive API actions when the user authorizes delete or close operations.
Token helper security should be reviewed before marketplace publication.

use_cases.title: Track Sprint Work
use_cases.description: List active projects, executions, assigned tasks, unresolved bugs, and blocked work from ZenTao.

use_cases.title: Maintain Product Backlog
use_cases.description: Create, update, close, or activate stories, epics, requirements, product plans, and releases.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Testing
use_cases.description: Manage bugs, test cases, test tasks, builds, and release readiness from ZenTao API data.

prompt_templates.title: List Active Project Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner query for read-only status review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my active ZenTao projects and executions. Include open tasks and unresolved bugs, grouped by project.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Confirmed Bug
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic write workflow with required fields.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ZenTao bug for product 12 titled Login fails after password reset. Use opened build trunk and severity 2. Ask me to confirm before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: Update Requirement Details
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate update that should fetch current data first.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update story 345 with the new acceptance criteria I provide. Fetch the current story first, keep unchanged fields, and confirm the final changes before submitting.

prompt_templates.title: Review Release Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced multi-module analysis across product, bug, build, and test data.
prompt_templates.prompt: For product 8, review release readiness. Check open bugs, active requirements, builds, test tasks, and tickets. Summarize blockers and recommended next actions.

output_examples.output: A grouped bug report with bug ID, title, severity, assignee, status, and next recommended action.

output_examples.input: Create a task in execution 18 for API regression testing.
output_examples.output: A confirmation message showing the task name, execution, assignee, estimate, and fields that will be submitted.

output_examples.input: Close story 122 because it is done.
output_examples.output: A status update confirming the close reason, affected story, API result, and any server warning returned by ZenTao.

best_practices: Use environment variables for ZenTao URL and credentials, and rotate tokens after shared machine use.
Confirm every create, update, delete, close, resolve, or activate action before sending the request.
Fetch current record details before PUT updates so unchanged required fields are preserved.
anti_patterns: Do not publish the current token helper without replacing eval output with shell-safe exports.
Do not store long-lived ZenTao tokens in shared home directories without strict file permissions.
Do not issue delete or close requests from vague prompts that lack item IDs and confirmation.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It connects to a ZenTao server through REST API v2.0 using a URL and token or login credentials.

faq.question: Can it change ZenTao records?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create, update, delete, close, resolve, and activate supported ZenTao records when authorized.

faq.question: Does it need a ZenTao token?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs ZENTAO_TOKEN or account and password values that can obtain a token.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The documented eval workflow can execute unescaped token helper output, and the helper stores long-lived tokens in plaintext.

faq.question: Were prompt injection attempts found?
faq.answer: No evidence found of prompt injection text in the reviewed skill files.

faq.question: Is this ready for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: No. The token helper should be fixed and reviewed before publication.
